skill.name: planning-guidelines
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents through conservative feature planning for Portfolio Buddy 2, with emphasis on code preservation, responsive UI, clarifying questions, and low-risk improvements.
skill.summary: Plan Portfolio Buddy 2 changes with preservation, responsiveness, and clear trade-off analysis.
skill.author: 5MinFutures

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but AI review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline code spans, and planning prose. No executable code, network access, filesystem access, environment access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution. In context, these locations are Markdown fences and inline code spans for TypeScript, TSX, Tailwind classes, and skill names. They do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters are inside Markdown documentation examples, not Ruby or shell source files. No command invocation syntax or executable wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alerts Match Non-Crypto Text
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography alerts appear on product description, responsive design guidance, trading-day examples, and summary text. No cryptographic API, cipher, hashing function, key handling, or security algorithm appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain user-facing planning guidance and UI examples. I found no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive algorithm in those contexts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Planning Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts occur in guidance about ambiguous requirements and avoiding unnecessary complexity. They do not inspect host state, enumerate systems, or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are ordinary prose in a planning checklist. There is no code path, command, or instruction to perform system discovery.

content.value_statement: Feature changes can create regressions when requirements are unclear or existing code is replaced too quickly. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan conservative, responsive changes with clear user approval points.
actual_capabilities: Defines a preserve-first strategy for Portfolio Buddy 2 feature work.
Provides criteria for when a better implementation should be proposed.
Gives a structured format for clarifying questions with recommended options.
Lists mobile and desktop checks for responsive UI changes.
Explains how to coordinate with related Portfolio Buddy 2 skills.
Includes concrete planning examples from portfolio analysis features.
limitations: It is tailored to Portfolio Buddy 2 and may need adaptation for other projects.
It provides planning guidance, not executable implementation code.
It depends on separate architecture and coding-standard skills for full context.
It does not verify current repository state or run tests by itself.

use_cases.title: Plan a Small Feature Change
use_cases.description: Use the skill before adding a focused Portfolio Buddy 2 feature so the agent extends existing code and checks both viewports.

use_cases.title: Clarify Ambiguous Requirements
use_cases.description: Use the question framework to present clear implementation options with trade-offs before work begins.

use_cases.title: Review a Refactor Proposal
use_cases.description: Use the exception criteria to decide whether a suggested refactor has low risk, high value, and acceptable complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a small, well-defined change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-guidelines skill to plan this Portfolio Buddy 2 fix. Preserve existing behavior, identify likely files, and list mobile and desktop checks.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Clarifying Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt when requirements have several valid interpretations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-guidelines skill to ask clarifying questions for this request. Present recommended and alternative approaches with pros, cons, and trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Assess an Improvement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for deciding whether to improve existing code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-guidelines skill to evaluate whether this proposed improvement meets the low-risk, high-upside, low-complexity criteria before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Full Feature Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for larger feature work that needs other project skills.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use planning-guidelines with the related Portfolio Buddy 2 skills. Create a feature plan covering requirements, architecture fit, coding standards, tests, and tracking updates.

output_examples.input: Add a Set to Total Margin button beside Starting Capital.
output: The plan preserves the existing input and adds one adjacent action.
Mobile layout uses compact spacing and hides long button text when needed.
Desktop layout keeps the label visible and aligns with existing controls.

output_examples.input: Trading Days should change when a date filter changes.
output_examples.output: The skill would ask whether Trading Days should mean calendar days or actual days with trades, then explain the trade-offs before coding.

output_examples.input: Replace the portfolio summary component with a new implementation.
output_examples.output: The skill would challenge the replacement and require evidence that the change is isolated, valuable, simple, and approved.

best_practices: Load architecture context before planning changes that affect shared components.
Ask clarifying questions before making product or financial calculation decisions.
Verify every UI plan against mobile and desktop layouts before implementation.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to justify broad refactors without user approval.
Skipping clarifying questions when several valid approaches exist.
Treating examples as current source code without checking the repository.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding agents plan Portfolio Buddy 2 changes while preserving existing code and responsive behavior.

faq.question: Does it write code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides planning decisions and approval points before code changes are made.

faq.question: Can it be used outside Portfolio Buddy 2?
faq.answer: Yes, but some guidance references Portfolio Buddy 2 files, examples, and companion skills.

faq.question: How does it handle unclear requirements?
faq.answer: It asks structured clarifying questions with a recommended option and fair alternatives.

faq.question: When does it allow refactoring?
faq.answer: It allows refactoring only when the change is low risk, high value, simple, and approved.

faq.question: Why does it mention mobile and desktop?
faq.answer: Portfolio Buddy 2 features must work well across small and large viewports.
